title: Telethon

jokes.text: What, and shoot him?
jokes.setup: Ron tells Andy to take the puppy outside
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately jumps to the darkest possible interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - innocent instruction becomes dramatic life-or-death scenario. Economy is excellent, character integration strong.

jokes.text: Oh, come on, Ron. I'm just a little puppy. I ain't done nothing wrong. I'm just a puppy.
jokes.setup: Andy speaking for the puppy after the 'shoot him' misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Andy gives the puppy a pleading voice and personality
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - anthropomorphizing the puppy with childlike innocence. Strong character moment.

jokes.text: I like your mustache. I wish I could have one. But I can't. Because I'm just a little puppy.
jokes.setup: Continuing to speak as the puppy
jokes.punchline: Puppy compliments Ron's mustache but can't grow one due to species limitation
jokes.explanation: Escalates the puppy bit beautifully. The mustache compliment is unexpected and the species logic is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Okay, take him out and shoot him.
jokes.setup: Andy's continued puppy voice annoying Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron deadpan agrees to Andy's dramatic interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response - dry, matter-of-fact agreement to the absurd. Economy and character integration are exceptional.

jokes.text: It goes us, Dallas, Tulsa and certain parts of The Mall of America.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining Pawnee is the fourth-fattest town
jokes.punchline: Including 'certain parts of The Mall of America' as a geographical location
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on ranking - treating mall sections as cities. Good specificity and unexpected geographical absurdity.

jokes.text: Gross! No! It's Jerry's sex night. That ruins sex and tonight.
jokes.setup: Jerry mentions it's a big night with his kids away
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horrified reaction to realizing what Jerry means
jokes.explanation: Leslie's visceral reaction is perfectly in character. 'That ruins sex and tonight' is economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Diabetes. Let's Dia-beat-this.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing off her homemade t-shirts
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun combining diabetes with 'beat this'
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad pun that's perfect for Leslie's over-enthusiastic character. The cringe is the point.

jokes.text: Yeah. Four hours to come up with the slogan. Four hours to embroider them. Time well spent.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her preparation
jokes.punchline: Eight hours total for a terrible pun and basic embroidery
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's misplaced priorities and dedication to terrible ideas. Good character work.

jokes.text: Salad sucks. There. I said it.
jokes.setup: Random confession moment
jokes.punchline: Andy treats anti-salad stance as brave declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - making mundane food opinion sound like courageous truth-telling. Great economy.

jokes.text: No, you should ask her in a hot air balloon. No. She should be on the hot air balloon, and you should ride up on horseback.
jokes.setup: Mark asking for proposal advice
jokes.punchline: Leslie's increasingly elaborate and impractical scenarios
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-planning. The escalation from simple to completely impractical is well-structured.

jokes.text: Definitely. Can you get five eagles? No, get ten eagles.
jokes.setup: Continuing proposal advice
jokes.punchline: Eagles as casual proposal accessories, then doubling down
jokes.explanation: The specificity of eagles and immediate escalation to ten is perfectly Leslie. Great quotable absurdity.

jokes.text: I can't make it to the telethon tonight, because I have no interest in being there.
jokes.setup: Expected excuse for missing work event
jokes.punchline: Completely honest admission of disinterest
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation of fake excuse with brutal honesty. Perfect Tom character moment with great economy.

jokes.text: Ex-Indiana Pacers small forward, Detlef Schrempf. The Detlef Schrempf?
jokes.setup: Leslie's big celebrity reveal
jokes.punchline: Treating obscure retired basketball player as major celebrity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pawnee celebrity - someone genuinely notable but hardly A-list. Tom's excited reaction sells it.

jokes.text: Well, they have a communal lipstick and a box of combs.
jokes.setup: Tom asking if cable access has hair and makeup
jokes.punchline: Pathetic description of 'professional' makeup facilities
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in describing low-budget operation. 'Communal lipstick' is particularly gross/funny.

jokes.text: 'The devil knows where you're hiding.' 'If you take enough rides with the devil, pretty soon, he's going to drive.' She was really into the devil.
jokes.setup: Leslie sharing her grandmother's sayings
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dark devil-focused wisdom, then understated conclusion
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from folksy wisdom to devil obsession. The deadpan conclusion is perfect. Very quotable.

jokes.text: That's horrible. I have to nap up. If I don't get a solid five, it kills my sunny disposition.
jokes.setup: Donna being assigned 4-6 AM shift
jokes.punchline: Donna claiming to have a sunny disposition that needs protection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna - the idea that she has a 'sunny disposition' is hilariously false given her personality.

jokes.text: Just like everybody knows you appeared in two episodes of the German soap opera Gute Zeiten Schlechte Zeiten.
jokes.setup: Tom pretending everyone knows Schrempf trivia
jokes.punchline: Obscure German soap opera appearances as 'common knowledge'
jokes.explanation: Tom's desperate attempt to impress backfires hilariously. The specificity of German soap opera is great.

jokes.text: Huh? Oh, 6'10". I'm 5'6" and three quarters.
jokes.setup: Height difference between Tom and Schrempf
jokes.punchline: Tom's oddly specific height measurement while clearly much shorter
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of height difference enhanced by Tom's precise measurement of his shortness.

jokes.text: My phone has 25 lines. I think it might be a switchboard.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to figure out his phone setup
jokes.punchline: Jerry slowly realizing he has the most complex equipment
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry getting the complicated assignment. Mild but fits his character of being overwhelmed by simple tasks.

jokes.text: And I am not a crook. No, he's not.
jokes.setup: Joan introducing herself on local TV
jokes.punchline: Random Nixon reference with cameraman confirmation
jokes.explanation: Random historical reference that shows Joan's odd professionalism. The confirmation makes it weirder.

jokes.text: Tonight, we're hoping the people of Pawnee dig their big, chubby hands into their plus-size pockets and donate generously.
jokes.setup: Appeal for donations to diabetes telethon
jokes.punchline: Insulting the audience while asking for money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joan - inappropriate commentary disguised as fundraising appeal. The directness is cringily effective.

jokes.text: Sugar. It's a block of sugar.
jokes.setup: Leslie wondering about the 'secret ingredient' in energy bars
jokes.punchline: Ann's matter-of-fact medical assessment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Leslie's mystery. Ann's medical knowledge as straight-man response.

jokes.text: Tigers.
jokes.setup: Ann trying to wake sugar-crashed Leslie
jokes.punchline: Leslie's random one-word response
jokes.explanation: Random word showing Leslie's disorientation. Simple but economical way to show her state.

jokes.text: The Snakehole is booming! People are loving Detlef Schrempf. I had no idea professional athletes were so popular.
jokes.setup: Tom supposed to bring Schrempf to telethon
jokes.punchline: Tom's fake surprise that athletes are popular
jokes.explanation: Tom's transparent excuse-making and fake naivety about athlete popularity. Decent character work.

jokes.text: We're going to get married and I'm pregnant with his child.
jokes.setup: Joan asking about April and Andy's relationship
jokes.punchline: April's completely false deadpan response on live TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - deadpan lies on live TV just to mess with people. Great character integration and delivery.

jokes.text: There are two types of diabetes, but only one type of caring. Type One caring.
jokes.setup: Leslie's earnest telethon introduction
jokes.punchline: Forced medical terminology pun that doesn't quite work
jokes.explanation: Another intentionally awkward Leslie pun. The medical reference is forced but fits her over-prepared personality.

jokes.text: You've got sex hair / You got it from me, girl. Sex hair
jokes.setup: Andy performing on telethon
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate song lyrics for diabetes fundraiser
jokes.explanation: Andy's complete inappropriateness for the venue. The cringe is the point - wrong song, wrong time.

jokes.text: Oh, I called him. He's in Liberia visiting my uncle. He wants to know what happened on Lost.
jokes.setup: Donna on phone during telethon
jokes.punchline: International call to discuss TV shows instead of working
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna priorities - using work time for international family TV recaps. Good escalation.

jokes.text: The ultimate celebrity, I think, to hang out with for a night would be Criss Angel. You'd be talking to him, and then, he would just turn into fire.
jokes.setup: Tom bonding with Schrempf about celebrities
jokes.punchline: Tom's shallow understanding of magic as literal transformation
jokes.explanation: Tom's superficial celebrity worship combined with literal interpretation of magic. Good character specificity.

jokes.text: I need you to make that out, 'To Wendy. Tom is an amazing guy. You never should have left him. You made a huge mistake in your life, and you're probably going to die alone. Love, Detlef.'
jokes.setup: Tom asking Schrempf for autograph
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bitter message to ex-wife disguised as fan autograph
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from simple autograph to passive-aggressive relationship revenge. Perfect Tom pathetic-ness.

jokes.text: Give a man a fish, and you feed him for a day. Don't teach a man to fish, and you feed yourself. He's a grown man. Fishing is not that hard.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his charity philosophy
jokes.punchline: Selfish twist on classic proverb with practical justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy - subverts familiar saying with selfish logic that somehow makes sense. Highly quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Over. Under. And you guessed it. Over.
jokes.setup: Elderly man demonstrating chair caning on TV
jokes.punchline: Treating mundane craft technique like exciting television
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - boring demonstration treated as riveting TV. The enthusiasm for mundane craft is funny.

jokes.text: Hey, why don't you forget about the donation and just come down here and meet me in person.
jokes.setup: April supposed to be taking donations
jokes.punchline: April trying to meet random caller instead of raising money
jokes.explanation: Classic April subversion of job duties for personal entertainment. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Have you ever seen this man sleep? It's like underwater ballet.
jokes.setup: Leslie looking at sleeping Mark
jokes.punchline: Oddly poetic description of Mark's sleeping style
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly beautiful simile for sleep. Leslie's romantic observation is sweet and specific.

jokes.text: Don't you remember when Ahmad Rashad proposed to Mrs. Cosby on TV? Most women do.
jokes.setup: Leslie convincing Mark to propose on TV
jokes.punchline: Obscure TV reference treated as universal female knowledge
jokes.explanation: Leslie's confident assertion about obscure TV moment. Good character touch showing her media knowledge.

jokes.text: Was she on the Titanic? No. She was just my grandma.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking about ring's story
jokes.punchline: Mark's matter-of-fact deflation of expected dramatic backstory
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expected family heirloom drama. Mark's straightforward response deflates Leslie's romanticism.

jokes.text: Listen, my van's out back. Let's roll. No. Please leave. Where are you going? Is this guy bothering you? No, I'm bothering you. For bothering her.
jokes.setup: Creepy caller shows up to meet April
jokes.punchline: Andy's confused protective instincts and garbled threat
jokes.explanation: Andy's protective but confused response. The mangled threat logic is very Andy-like.

jokes.text: I work for the Sewage Department. I'm up to my waist in hot snizz.
jokes.setup: Creepy guy trying to impress
jokes.punchline: Disgusting job description with inappropriate slang
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that establishes character as genuinely creepy. The wordplay is juvenile but effective for the character.

jokes.text: I will now drink eight glasses of milk in three minutes. No. No, you won't. No, you won't. Because if you do that, you will die.
jokes.setup: Jerry's desperate attempt to fill airtime
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact death prediction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Jerry's mundane stunt to Leslie's dire health warning. The certainty of death is funny.

jokes.text: No. What we're going to do is we're going to pull our pants down. That's what we're going to do. Everyone's gonna pull their pants down for diabetes.
jokes.setup: Leslie desperately trying to create TV moment
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate solution to boring television
jokes.explanation: Leslie's complete desperation leading to wildly inappropriate live TV moment. Good cringe escalation.

jokes.text: You just love flashing your ass, don't you? When it's for a good cause, Joan.
jokes.setup: Joan commenting on Leslie's pants situation
jokes.punchline: Leslie's earnest justification for inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - turning embarrassing moment into charitable mission. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: By the way, I'm drunk.
jokes.setup: Detlef making professional telethon appearance
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of intoxication on live TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and delivery. The casual nature of the admission makes it funnier. Great economy.

jokes.text: And the story of this next dance is that it's called The Worm.
jokes.setup: News anchor doing dance segment
jokes.punchline: Perd's characteristic over-explanation of obvious things
jokes.explanation: Perfect Perd - unnecessarily explaining what everyone can see. 'The story of this dance' is characteristically verbose.

jokes.text: What the (fuck) are you doing, Perd Hapley?
jokes.setup: Ron watching Perd's dance performance
jokes.punchline: Ron's rare display of confusion and profanity
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Ron being genuinely flummoxed. The full name usage and profanity show his complete bewilderment.

jokes.text: So, I may have parked on your front lawn. You did. I did.
jokes.setup: Leslie visiting Ann while exhausted
jokes.punchline: Casual admission and confirmation of property damage
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's exhausted state through poor decision-making. The matter-of-fact exchange is well-crafted.

jokes.text: And she's been asleep for twenty-two hours.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining Leslie fell asleep on her couch
jokes.punchline: Reveal of impossibly long sleep duration
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Leslie's exhaustion storyline. The specific duration makes the crash funnier.

jokes.text: But I think I'm going to draw a mustache on her face.
jokes.setup: Ann expressing love for sleeping Leslie
jokes.punchline: Immediate turn to harmless but mischievous prank
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment undercut by harmless mischief. Shows Ann's playful side emerging around Leslie's influence.

tmdb_overview: Leslie hosts a late-night shift for a local telethon, and lands former Indiana Pacer Detlef Schrempf as a celebrity guest. Meanwhile, Mark has big plans for his relationship with Ann.
insight: Telethon sustains 80+ laughs through pure character escalation across 47 gags in 25 minutes.
